data.title: Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals shares surge 3.25%, among top Midcap gainers today
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

During today's session, shares of Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals experienced a notable increase, rising by 3.25% to reach Rs 138.59. This movement positions Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals among the top gainers within the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index.

Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals' financial performance reveals the following trends:

Quarterly Performance:

  • March 2025: Revenue stood at Rs 24,595.87 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 363.14 Cr and an EPS of Rs 2.11.
  • December 2024: Revenue was Rs 21,870.86 Cr, net profit at Rs 304.19 Cr, and EPS at Rs 1.76.
  • September 2024: The company recorded revenue of Rs 24,967.87 Cr but experienced a net loss of Rs -704.82 Cr, resulting in an EPS of -3.98.

Annual Performance:
  • March 2025: Revenue reached Rs 94,681.62 Cr, although net profit was comparatively low at Rs 28.08 Cr, leading to an EPS of Rs 0.32.
  • March 2024: Revenue was Rs 90,406.68 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 3,582.44 Cr and an EPS of Rs 20.52.

Balance Sheet Highlights (March 2025):
  • Share Capital: Rs 1,752 Cr
  • Reserves & Surplus: Rs 11,216 Cr
  • Total Assets: Rs 34,434 Cr

Key Financial Ratios (March 2025):
  • Basic EPS: Rs 0.32
  • Book Value per Share: Rs 74.00
  • Debt to Equity Ratio: 0.99

Cash Flow Analysis (March 2025):
  • Cash Flow from Operating Activities: Rs 1,877 Cr
  • Cash Flow from Investing Activities: -Rs 939 Cr
  • Cash Flow from Financing Activities: -Rs 937 Cr

The financials indicate a fluctuating quarterly performance, with a stronger annual revenue but a decrease in net profit in the most recent year. The company maintains a substantial asset base and manages its debt reasonably, as reflected in its debt-to-equity ratio.

The recent sentiment analysis from Moneycontrol suggests a very bearish outlook as of May 8, 2025, which contrasts with today's stock price increase. Mangalore Refinery and Petrochemicals has shown a price increase during today's trading session, despite a bearish sentiment analysis.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Maruti Suzuki India Shares Fall 0.5% in Today's Trading Session
data.description:
Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

During today's trading session, Maruti Suzuki India experienced a slight decrease, with its shares falling by 0.51% to a price of Rs 12605.00. This movement reflects ongoing market dynamics for the NIFTY 50 STOCKS-listed company.

Financial Performance Overview

Maruti Suzuki India's financial performance showcases a consistent pattern of revenue and profit generation over the recent quarters and years.

Quarterly Highlights (Fiscal Year 2024-25):

  • March 2025 Quarter: Revenue reached Rs 40,920.10 Cr, an increase from Rs 38,764.30 Cr in December 2024.
  • Net Profit: The company reported a net profit of Rs 3,839.20 Cr for the March quarter, slightly down from Rs 3,659.80 Cr in the previous quarter.
  • EPS (Earnings Per Share): EPS stood at 124.40 in March 2025, marginally up from 118.54 in December 2024.
Annual Performance:

Over the past five years, Maruti Suzuki India has demonstrated substantial growth in both revenue and net profit:

  • Revenue: Increased from Rs 70,372.00 Cr in 2021 to Rs 152,913.00 Cr in 2025.
  • Net Profit: Rose from Rs 4,220.10 Cr in 2021 to Rs 14,256.30 Cr in 2025.
  • EPS: Improved from 145.30 in 2021 to 461.20 in 2025.

The NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index is trading at 20,677.85 during today's session, with a gain of 0.19 percent, equivalent to 39.95 points. The Nifty Midcap 150 Index opened at 20,712.05, reaching a high of 20,720.10 and a low of 20,614.45. The 52 Week High for the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 is 22,515.40 and the 52 Week Low is 17,269.50.

The NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index has demonstrated the following returns: a YTD return of -2.19 percent, a 1-week return of 5.54 percent, and a 1-month return of 7.82 percent. The index has also shown returns of 12.71 percent over 3 months, 2.85 percent over 6 months and 9.61 percent over the past year. Furthermore, the Nifty Midcap 150 Index has provided returns of 68.60 percent over the past two years and 103.49 percent over the past three years.

Technical indicators for the NIFTY MIDCAP 150 index during today's session are as follows: SMA50 is 19,112.66, and the SMA200 is 20,497.07. The RSI (14) is at 64.61. The MACD (12,26,9) is 342.13.
